issues.description: Claim 'roughly ten to fifteen percent of the AP exam' is a specific numerical weighting for Unit 4. The reference excerpt (lines 228-289) does NOT contain any exam-weighting percentage. Flag as unsupported by the provided reference.
Specific number '3.8 million new cells every second' is not present anywhere in the reference. Flag as unsupported.
Names 'separase' and 'cohesin' are used. The reference (line 251-252) does say 'cohesin proteins cleaved by separase' — so both terms ARE supported. No flag. (Recording the trace.)

Claim that p53 is 'mutated in over half of all human cancers' includes a specific statistic. The reference (line 285-286) states 'Mutated in >50% of human cancers' — this IS supported. No flag. However, the script also calls p53 the 'guardian of the genome,' which the reference also supports (line 284). Both supported.
'MPF, M-phase Promoting Factor — is cyclin B bound to CDK1' — reference line 274 confirms 'MPF (M-phase Promoting Factor) = cyclin B + CDK1; triggers entry into mitosis.' Supported. No flag.
Specific gene names 'Ras and Myc' as proto-oncogenes and 'p53 and Rb' as tumor suppressors — reference lines 278-282 list exactly these examples. Supported. No flag.

Specific resource names 'OpenStax Biology, Chapter Ten' and 'Khan Academy AP Biology Unit Four problem sets' are external citations that do NOT appear in the reference excerpt. These are study-resource pointers rather than scientific claims, but as specific named sources they are unsupported by the provided reference.

summary: Traced approximately 22 specific claims across the 16 sections (gene names, phase names, structures, percentages, protein identities, resource names). Confirmed support for 19 (e.g., cohesin/separase, MPF=cyclinB+CDK1, p53>50% cancers, Ras/Myc/p53/Rb, cleavage furrow, cell plate, G0 contains neurons and mature muscle cells, restriction-point label, all five mitosis phase names and events). Flagged 3 unsupported specific claims: the '10-15%' exam-weighting figure, the '3.8 million cells per second' figure, and the named external study resources (OpenStax Ch. 10, Khan Academy). Reviewer has no subject expertise; flags reflect reference coverage only.
